title: Infosys McCamish Enters Vermont Consent Order Over Cyber Incident Disclosure Allegations
detailed_summary: Infosys disclosed that Infosys McCamish Systems ('IMS'), a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into a Stipulation and Consent Order with the State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation on July 9, 2025. The DFR had alleged violations of the Vermont Security Breach Notice Act related to IMS's response to information requests during the regulator's cybersecurity event investigation and alleged failures to provide timely and accurate notice to data owners. IMS resolved the matter without a hearing and without admitting the alleged violations. IMS is required to pay an administrative penalty of USD 125,000 within 30 calendar days of entry of the order by the Commissioner.

key_facts.fact: On July 9, 2025, Infosys McCamish Systems ('IMS'), a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into a Stipulation and Consent Order with the State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The DFR had alleged violations of the Vermont Security Breach Notice Act by IMS related to the cybersecurity event.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The DFR alleged that IMS failed to respond to its request for information with timely and accurate information and records and failed to provide timely and accurate notice of the cybersecurity event to data owners.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: IMS entered into the Stipulation and Consent Order to resolve the matter without a hearing, and the order records that IMS does not admit the existence of the alleged violations.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: IMS is required to pay an administrative penalty of USD 125,000 within 30 calendar days of entry of the Stipulation and Consent Order by the Commissioner.
